truth.papers.A56.paper_scope: Analysis of channel segregates formation in castings, emphasizing the role of defects in the dendrite network and a criterion for occurrence.

prediction.papers.A56.paper_scope: Theoretical analysis of the formation conditions for channel segregates in dendritic networks during solidification, focusing on the role of small permeability defects and the direction of interdendritic flow relative to isotherms.

prediction.computational_details.idx_0.analytical_m: Percolation model of interdendritic flow with ellipsoidal defect approximation
Derivation of criterion [10] for channel segregate formation assisted by small defects

The model implicitly uses a percolation picture for interdendritic flow and approximates defects as ellipsoidal regions of different permeability. The approximations make the derivation only semiquantitatively accurate, and the percolation model is inaccurate at high fractions liquid. Values for the constant n in equation [10] are difficult to measure.

truth.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: The growth of channel segregates is greatly assisted by the presence of small defects in the dendrite network.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The condition [10] should provide a more reliable indication of the occurrence of channel segregates than [1].
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Channel segregates may form in situations where the interdendritic flow direction is substantially parallel to isotherms, because defects oriented out of the plane of the isotherms can divert interdendritic flow to a direction which will lead to channel segregation.
truth.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: The approximations made in the derivation of [10] make it only semiquantitatively accurate.

prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_0.value: Channel segregates are expected to form most readily at high fractions liquid.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_1.value: The growth of channel segregates is greatly assisted by the presence of small defects in the dendrite network; since such defects are unavoidable in real castings, condition [10] should provide a more reliable indication of channel segregate occurrence than condition [1].
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_2.value: Channel segregates may form in situations where the interdendritic flow direction is substantially parallel to isotherms, because defects oriented out of the plane of the isotherms can divert interdendritic flow to a direction leading to channel segregation.
prediction.unmapped_findings.idx_3.value: This research was supported by the National Science Foundation under grant DMR 79-24268 and by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration under grant NSG 7645.
